name: Rusty Patched Bumblebee
title: Collaborator
description: The Rusty Patched Bumblebee is a famously social insect, and highly cooperative with others. Especially when defending its hive, where it resides the entirety of its life, it can get defensive.
understandResult: Division of tasks and systems are crucial to the life of a bumblebee. You are organized and enjoy having a schedule.
A eusocial species, bees have some of the most complex social structures found in the animal kingdom. You lead with your heart, and want to make choices that are in the best interest of everyone.
Rusty Patched Bees prefer to warn other animals before they resort to stinging. They are deeply connected with their hive. You enjoy socializing and meeting new people.
Once a hive is established, foragers are the only ones who depart regularly, flying up to one mile for food. You are grounded, and focus on concrete details.
Bees' behavior is entirely defensive, and they are more likely to take flight than fight. You can be conflict avoidant, and sometimes stress can get the better of you.

facts.General Facts: The most famous social insect, this species is one of 21 in North America, and has faced huge loss of habitat.
facts.Physical Description: What sets the rusty patched bumblebee apart is, you guessed it, a rusty patch on the abdomen of their worker bees and males. Like other bumblebee species, they have a round body (thorax) covered entirely in yellow and black hairs.
facts.Predator or Prey?: Bees as a species are generalist foragers, and collect pollen and nectar from a range of flowering plants. This species has a notably wide range of plants it collects from relative to other bees. With their pollen and nectar collected, worker bees return to the hive, where a complex network of helpers turns their stomach contents into food. The rusty patched bumblebee does not produce honey, but they serve as an important pollinator that benefits any ecosystem lucky enough to host them.
facts.Community and Trust: A eusocial species, rusty patched bees have the most complex type of social structure found in the animal kingdom. Examples of their social behavior include living with many generations, dividing labor into different roles, and raising their young as a group.
facts.Assertiveness: Bumblebees prefer not to sting or engage in conflict. When predators or intruders approach, they will first try to warn them before resorting to stinging. This type of bee is able to sting multiple times and does not lose its stinger. They are extremely social interspecies as well. Does the Rusty Patched Bumblebee Migrate? Like other bees, they nest underground or in clumps of grass. Wild nests sustain up to 1,000 members. Each winter, the hive is killed off, only to be restarted each spring by the queen, who hibernates through the winter. Once a hive is established, the only migration that occurs is the foragers, who fly up to one mile from their hive for food.
facts.Food Chain: Bumblebees are prey to a wide variety of species, including birds, larger stinging insects, spiders, and even badgers, who can eat the entire hive in one swoop. That being the case, their behavior is entirely defensive, and they are more likely to take flight than fight.
facts.Endangered Status: The rusty patched bumblebee was the first bumblebee to be declared endangered in the United States under the Endangered Species Act. They were once dominant across the east coast of the US, but have experienced a huge loss of population and habitat. By some estimates, they've lost 83% of their original population. Protecting their homes and reducing the impact of climate change are the best ways to allow these important pollinators to keep benefitting their ecosystem.

charityDesc: The National Wildlife Federation preserves the United State's natural landscapes for future generations of people and endangered species.
